verses.commentary: Give me children or I'll die! Desperate demand.
Am I in the place of God? Who has kept you from children? He recognizes divine sovereignty over the womb.
Surrogacy solution, echoing Sarah and Hagar (16:2). Building family through another woman's body.
Polygamy expands.
Surrogacy works. Rachel's solution succeeds where her body failed.
God vindicated me.
Second son through Bilhah. Proxy motherhood continues. Rachel's desperation mounts.
Great struggle (naftulei Elohim, נַפְתּוּלֵי אֱלֹהִים), wrestlings of God. Naming claims victory. Sibling rivalry intensifies.
Leah's countermove. Now four wives.
Leah responds through Zilpah. Escalating rivalry. Household divides further.
What good fortune (literally luck or troop).
Zilpah's second son.
How happy (be'oshri, בְּאָשְׁרִי).
Mandrakes, believed to aid fertility. Rachel negotiates with Leah. Desperate measures to gain what only God can give.
Will you take mandrakes too? Rachel bargains.
The transaction is explicit. Jacob becomes a commodity between them. He remains passive throughout this twisted arrangement.
God's intervention, not mandrakes. Fifth son comes through divine action, not folk remedies. Divine sovereignty trumps human schemes.
God rewarded me. She sees it as divine payment.
Sixth son. Leah is prolific.
God presented a precious gift. Six sons.
Daughter born. Named Dinah (דִּינָה), judgment.
God remembered Rachel. Divine timing, not human manipulation. God opens the womb on his schedule.
God has taken away my disgrace.
May the LORD add (yosef, יוֹסֵף) another son. The name Joseph is prophetic. Benjamin comes later (35:16-18).
Send me away. Go home.
After fourteen years of service, Jacob requests permission to leave. Legitimate plea for independence.

I have learned by divination that the LORD has blessed me because of you. Please stay. I have learned by divination (nachash, נָחַשׁ). LORD blessed me because of you. Laban recognizes Jacob brings blessing (like Joseph in Egypt, 39:5).
Laban opens negotiation. Name your wages. But Laban never deals fairly.
Your livestock fared well. Jacob's competence is clear.
The LORD blessed Laban because of Jacob. When may I provide for my household? Jacob wants to build his own future.
What shall I give you? Don't give me anything.
Let me go through all your flocks today and remove from them every speckled or spotted sheep, every dark-colored lamb and every spotted or speckled goat.
Any goat in my possession that is not speckled or spotted, or any lamb that is not dark-colored, will be considered stolen.
He thinks he's getting the better deal.
He preemptively takes what Jacob should get.
Making it harder for Jacob to breed marked animals.
This seems like folk magic but God is actually blessing Jacob (31:9-12).
Breeding strategy.
The result: marked offspring.
Jacob separated. Separate flocks.
Placed branches. Selective breeding.
Weak to Laban. Strong to Jacob.
God blesses Jacob despite Laban's deception.
